look.text: On the counter, wrapped in wax paper: a whole marble rye. Nib bakes it himself and dares the world to comment.

take.text: You palm the marble rye off the counter with the fluid ease of a man who owes this establishment nine dollars.

look.text: The board now reads WINNERS: BOOTH 4. That's you. That's Kessler and a rye. History is written by whoever's least hungry at the buzzer.

TONIGHT'S TRIVIA — GRAND PRIZE $3. Tonight's category: 'Years Things Happened.' The question chalked below: "In what year did the ballpoint pen go on sale?" You have absolutely no idea. Someone in your life definitely does.

use.text: You've already won. Gloating further would be gauche, and you're saving all your gauche for the jacket.
You march up, Kessler in tow, and answer: NINETEEN OH FOUR. The buzzer AGREES. Three dollars, and the pure clean joy of being right about something that does not matter at all.
The buzzer waits. You do not know when the ballpoint pen went on sale. You barely know what year it is NOW. You need someone who knows things.
You know the answer, but the pot pays a TEAM. You need a trivia-brain standing next to you at the buzzer.

look.text: Nib. Apron, spatula, a ledger where your name appears in a color he reserves for the guilty.

talk.text: You lay nine dollars on the counter — every cent of it schemed. "There. The tab. Paid. Nobody mention this again." Nib reaches, slowly, for the ledger...

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: "Put my rye back or eat it and add it to the tab. Those are the options. There is no third rye option."

look.text: Elna, in Booth 4, doing the crossword in pen and correcting the newspaper's grammar in the margins.
